And for me, having a word like spectacular means that I am looking for the spectacular every single day. I can tell you how this showed up for me on the weekend. I have a bike. I will ask Adam and remind and let you all know. But she makes me happy. Beatrice. Beatrice the bike. She brings me joy. But I. I've forgotten to bring these little tiny things back into my everyday because I allowed my life to run me instead of me running my life. So word for me is very important.

For Adam though, he needed a goal. You all know that he is working very hard to get his car on the road and run 11 second passes. That's his aim for this year. Why is that significant to him? Because every single hour he does extra, he's putting money into the kitty for him to go and buy.

He's just received his custom made camshaft, many thousands of dollars more than he thought he was going to spend. So when he's working on a 42 degree day and his body's cramping up and he feels like he's going to pass out, it's the thing that helps him push that little bit harder to get where he wants to go.
Because he's got a why, he's got a goal, he's got a thing he's working towards. So that was very significant, important to him.

Goals don't work so well for me. Intentions work well for me as well. But for this year, a word worked well. So we want you all to bring one of those as well. I encourage you to come as a couple to these if you can, even if your significant other doesn't work in the business. Um, we're talking about our life plan. What do we want from our life in the next 10 years? We break it down to five years, to three years, to two years and one year.

We can send you the worksheets beforehand so that you have the opportunity to get clarity on that together before so that you can then build out that plan. All right, question time. Okay, let's talk about burnout. I've got a couple of questions here this week about burnout. So I wanted to just talk about how burnout manifests and then talk about some things that have worked really well for many of our members, and including myself, to manage burnout.
First of all, I think it's important to acknowledge that we all have different capacity levels and we have different energy flows.

And so whilst burnout nearly always, well, overwhelming, burnout nearly always manifest themselves the same way. How and when we get there looks different for every single person because of the energy levels.

And so overwhelm tends to look like stress, snapping, moodiness, forgetfulness, a lack of clarity, and usually things like our regular daily discipline. So any kind of movement that we do, our diet, our drinking habits, all of those shift and change. So that's what you kind of.

You feel messy inside and you look messy to those that live with you every day on the outside because you are showing up differently now. How and when you get to that point looks different for everybody. And what point of that suggests overwhelm is also different for everybody.

I get really bad with forgetfulness, so juggling all the balls for me gets too much when I'm in overwhelm. And I forget stuff everywhere, which is why the system I'm about to talk about works particularly well. But for other people, like Adam, when he's in overwhelm, he does one or two things.
He retreats in on himself, and so you can't get a conversation out of him. And I don't know what's going on or he gets snappy. Depends on what's happening within his.

It will look different for all of you, but it is important that you get familiar with what the signs of overwhelm are so that you can work towards heading that off a little bit like a cash flow forecast. We want to be checking in with ourselves on a weekly basis.

We don't always recognise personally when we're in overwhelm, so we have to trust the people around us to be able to bring that to the table as a conversation so that we can then make plans to work around this. However, if you work with the system that I'm about to talk about, it can reduce it for most of us.

If not Eliminate it actually coming up for you. You just need to be mindful that it's coming. Okay. The system I wanted to talk about today is really basic. One that we've spoken about many, many times, but it's twofold. So it starts with a daily dump list.

The biggest problem with Overwhelm is we're carrying around too much information in our heads. It's impossible for us to have clarity when we are carrying too much stuff. It's like a big black cloud. I explained it this morning to somebody, like carrying a wheelbarrow of overload, an overloaded wheelbarrow of cement.

When we're in Overwhelm, we're either going to do one or two things that things going to fall over and tip out. If we're the apprentice, that's always going to happen. But for many of us, it will at this point, or it's going to start dripping out as we go because it's impossible for us to carry all of the stuff.
So if we take what's in our brain and get it down onto a list, then that's the first part of the process.

We're freeing up brain space. So think about it like a computer. You're freeing up RAM on your computer by deleting some of the information. In this case, I'm not asking for you to delete it. I'm just asking for you to put it down into a spot where you can go back to it. When Adam was at his worst in and around his breakdowns, he had one of those little Spyrex notebooks in his top pocket. That thing went everywhere. Pretty sure Shane has these, too.

Does he not, Lara? Yeah, he does. So carry around a notebook with you if need be. Use the notes function in your phone if need be, if you're in the office. Like, I have my list with me that is on my desk all of the time. Now, what do you do with your dump list when you've created dump lists?

Well, there's two things that we need to do. First is to prioritize. What is it that actually needs to be done? Because a lot of what we're carrying around doesn't need to be thought about just yet. It can be left over for another day or another time, but it's stuff that comes up because we're trying to manage so much.

And when we're starting to feel overwhelmed, we manage even tighter. We almost micromanage. So the idea of this is we get it out. We don't have to worry about it. We know that we get to go back to where that is. There's an account of it, but we want to prioritize what actually needs to be completed.

The second part of that and the most important part is what actually needs to be completed by me and what am I just trying to maintain control of? Because a lot of what we try to do as business owners is, and good humans, we don't actually need to do.
There are other people on our team or our partners or external support that can assist us with what's on our list.

We just try to do it all to protect the people around us. And the unfortunate part of that is we contribute to our own overwhelm and eventually our own breakdowns. So what I'd like you to do is assess your list using some highlighters, come up with a color coding system that works well for you.

Want you to look at having some colors and understanding what color is going to represent something that needs to be delegated. What color is something that's a non negotiable, you absolutely must do yourself because nobody else can.
And what color actually isn't important because so much of what's on your list will not be important. It can wait. And then I want you to have somebody else check your list.

Because the other thing I'm going to guarantee that you'll do as a business owner and a good human is you'll keep shouldering too much of the stuff that could be let go of. It's not about ego, it's about caring for the people around us. But what we do when we do this is we don't care for ourselves.

And eventually we can't help the people around us because it's either too much on our list or we've slipped into burnout. So to recap, it's a daily dump list. It's a delegation of the list to the best of our ability.
It's allowing somebody else to come in and contribute to that list to give you some feedback so that you can start to build out the structure to let it go.
The final, most important part for us when we're reaching into burnout, I think I said this earlier, is we, we tend to let go of the good habits and introduce bad habits.

So I want you to come back to your list of 10. So anyone new on the call that hasn't heard me talk about A list of 10 before? A list of 10 is a visual list of 10 things that make you feel good. Some should be small things that you can do that cost no money and take only moments of Time.

And some will be big things that you work towards. Look, Tara's got hers. Well done, Tara. So little ones for me are putting on lipstick. I always feel 10 times better when I have a face of makeup on and a bit of lipstick.

I just feel better about who I show up to be. Another thing that works really well for me that is free is 10 minutes in the sunshine with a cup of tea and bare feet in the grass. I feel amazing. Riding my bike is on the list. It just got ignored for five years. Going to the beach is on the list. A weekend away with friends is on the list. Bigger things that I need to work towards, they give me a goal, something to remind me.

I want you each to have your own list of 10. And I'd encourage you to post your list of 10 into the group to give others encouragement to do so. A list of 10 means that when you are in overwhelm and you cannot think of what it is that you can do for yourself, you've got a list of things you know make you feel good.

You can pick one and you can do it. We will always put ourselves last because we're good humans. And if you keep putting yourself last, you will end up in burnout. So have a list of 10. Lean into your list of 10.

Only needs to be one small thing a day for you to feel like you're giving back to yourself. Present prevents a lot of resentment that shows up when we're dealing with these emotions. I would encourage you to encourage your partners to do the same and hold each other accountable.

So if you remember back to above the line and below the line, this is part of being above the line. We're responsible for how we show up. We're responsible for our own behavior. Sometimes we need some support in that. So make each other accountable to this.
What have you done for yourself today? Have you shown up for you? Really, really important? Okay, I think that's talking to overwhelm.

I guess the other big part of this is the recognition and the understanding that the energy levels between partners and business can also create opportunity for overwhelm for one or the other. Because what it looks like to each other is, I need you to deal with this now.
But this other person's in overwhelm, and so they can't, which then creates resentment and frustration for the person that asked the question in the first place.

When we can recognize and understand that we all work differently, we all have different energy patterns, we all have different outcomes that we want to achieve, we can bring ourselves a Great deal of personal space because we're not trying to micromanage the other person.
So if I talk to Adam's example, poor old Adam, I promise he's okay with me talking about all this stuff. But Adam's example, when he was.

When we got through the other side of his burnout and then his breakdowns and having all that time off where he just wasn't able to show up in the business, once we got him back onto an even keel, I had to get out of the way.

I created a lot of what he was going through. I created a lot of what he was going through because I fixed all the problems. Every time there was a problem, I'd help him fix it. But fixing the problems didn't allow him to build the resilience so he could fix them himself.

So if you're in a relationship, we have those different energy levels, and they're showing up for you in business as frustration and resentment, and you're fixing things or making things happen or trying to create the perfect outcome, but your partner's not buying in or they're doing something very differently, then I encourage you to step aside and allow them to live through their own consequences.

Now, this is really scary because when you're running a business together, there's probably going to be a financial consequence that impacts both of you that you feel like you can capture or fix. But if they never feel that consequence, none of their behavior will ever change.
They'll never build out the resilience to do what is necessary to create the outcome that you're both working towards.

And I guess the final part of this is coming together for the life and wealth plan and knowing that you're planning for the same thing. Now, some of our couples don't want the same thing. I won't name them, but we do have a couple that are working for very different life goals.

And that's okay because the one goal they have in common is for their family. There's a particular type of lifestyle they're working towards for their family. Part of that is giving them the ability to achieve the things they want to achieve individually.
That makes them the best couple, the best family, the best version of themselves, because they still have the commonality of wanting a particular kind of lifestyle for their family.

If you're in a relationship where you're really, really different and you're finding that that showing up is not being able to come together with the common goal, with one common goal, then I encourage you to reach out to me.

Reach out to me I'll help you find it. It'll be there. We're also working on some tech support for some of this stuff for you guys for TIB 90. Okay, so list of 10 dump lists. Delegation. Getting some support with delegation.

They're going to make a massive impact on your day to day, particularly at the beginning of the year where we all tend to feel really overwhelmed. There's a lot of pressure and come to Life and Wealth plan so we can give you the clarity about what you're working towards.

What's the thing I need to action. What is it that this is trying to show me at this point? The sooner we get that, the sooner we can move through and we can go on that beautiful downhill slide for a little while. But rest assured, there's another hill coming.
It doesn't matter how big the business is or how perfect the business is.

There are going to be ebbs and flows and messy bits all the time because it's going to continue to evolve and change with you. So, yep, business is hard. Business feels really messy at times. I encourage you to lean on your support network. I we have the men's and ladies chats for you.

I really deeply encourage you to be vulnerable in those, to go and put your hand up in those and say, I am not coping. This is going on for me. It is amazing how many people in this community have the exact same experiences that each of you have had or are having.
